data.title: OpenAI Considers Handing 5% Stake to Trump Administration to Ease AI Pressure
data.description:
OpenAI, the creator of ChatGPT, is reportedly discussing a plan to give the Trump administration a 5% stake in the company. The proposal is part of an effort to ease growing concerns about artificial intelligence (AI) firms in Washington. If approved, the plan could also encourage other leading U.S. AI firms to offer similar stakes to the government.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Altman Proposes Giving 5% Stake of OpenAI to U.S. Govt 

Reports from the Financial Times, citing people familiar with the matter, revealed that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman proposed handing shares of the AI firm during early meetings with U.S. President Donald Trump and other senior officials. Under the plan, the administration would hold a 5% stake in OpenAI through a government investment vehicle rather than directly through federal agencies.

Based on OpenAI's $852 billion valuation following its March funding round, the stake would be worth about $42.6 billion. The proposal is also based on a model that would let Americans benefit from the long-term value created by AI. Neither OpenAI nor the White House has confirmed that a deal has been reached. 

OpenAI's Stake Plan May Expand to Rivals

OpenAI's proposal could also extend to other leading U.S. AI companies. Under the plan, Anthropic, the developer of Claude AI, and tech giants Google (GOOGL) and Meta Platforms (META), would each offer a similar stake through a sovereign wealth fund. However, it remains unclear whether any of the tech companies would agree to the proposal.

Notably, OpenAI's latest pitch follows more than a year of talks over a possible government stake in the firm. Reports last month said Altman first raised the idea with the Trump administration in early 2025. In April, OpenAI also proposed creating a public wealth fund to hold AI-related assets and share the gains with the public.

Can I Buy Shares in OpenAI?

OpenAI is a privately held company, so its shares are not available for public trading. However, the firm has confidentially filed initial public offering (IPO) draft papers, although no listing date or pricing has been announced. For more information ahead of OpenAI's IPO, investors can track its performance and new updates on the TipRanks Private Company Center.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: Apple(AAPL) Eyes Blacklisted China Chipmakers as AI Memory Shortage Pressures Margins
data.description:
Apple Inc. (AAPL), the iPhone maker, is reportedly in talks to buy memory chips from two Chinese semiconductor makers as it seeks relief from a global memory shortage that has driven component costs sharply higher.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

According to Bloomberg, Apple is negotiating with ChangXin Memory Technologies Inc. and Yangtze Memory Technologies Co., known as CXMT and YMTC, for chips that would be used in devices sold in China. The talks are still ongoing, and no final deal has been reached.

The move comes after Apple recently raised prices across Macs, iPads, home devices, and the Vision Pro, citing a rapid increase in memory-chip costs. AI demand has created a supply squeeze, as chipmakers shift more production toward high-margin data center products.

Meanwhile, AAPL shares rose 1.73% on Wednesday, closing at $294.38.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Google Stock Slides as it Loses €4.1B Fight Against Record European Android Fine
data.description:
Shares in tech titan Alphabet (GOOGL) were lower in pre-market trading today after it lost its battle against a record EU antitrust fine dating back almost a decade.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Android Blocking Claims

The European Commission handed out a €4.34 billion fine to Alphabet-owned Google in 2018 for its agreements which forced phone manufacturers to pre-install Google Search, the Chrome browser and the Google Play app store on their Android devices and prevented them from using rival ‌Android systems.

 A lower tribunal subsequently ⁠trimmed the fine to €4.1 billion in 2022 after Google challenged the EU penalty. Google then appealed ⁠to the Luxembourg-based Court of Justice of the European Union, Europe's highest.

However, the court has now sided with the EU antitrust watchdog.

"The appeal brought by Google and its parent company Alphabet ​against the ​judgment of the General Court is dismissed, ​thereby confirming the penalty imposed ‌for Google Search's abuse of a dominant position in the context of the Android operating system," judges said.

Google Focuses on Openness

A Google spokesperson said that the judgment failed to take into account its investment to ensure Android remains open, interoperable and free.

"In any event, we adapted our agreements to comply with the initial decision back in 2018 ‌and we remain focused on continued innovation and ​openness for our users, partners and developers", Google ​said.

The company and other U.S. tech titans continue to fall foul of Europe's drive to reduce their power on the continent. This is partly due to European governments wanting to bolster domestic companies, but also to be more independent of U.S. tech particularly with the Trump administration proving less friendly to Europe than previous American leaders.

Google alone has racked up close ​to €11 billion in EU fines in the last decades for ‌various antitrust infringements. One example came last year when the Commission slapped Google with a €2.95 billion for anti-competitive practices in its advertising technology business.

Is GOOGL a Good Stock to Buy Now?

On TipRanks, GOOGL has a Strong Buy consensus based on 28 Buy and 5 Hold ratings. Its highest price target is $515. GOOGL stock's consensus price target is $428.71, implying an 18.69% upside. (See GOOGL Stock Forecast).
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 7/2/26 – Futures Waver as Tech Rout Deepens, Jobs Data in Focus
data.description:
U.S. stock futures traded mixed ahead of Thursday's session as weakness in chip and AI-related stocks weighed on sentiment. Investors are also awaiting the June jobs report, with economists expecting the U.S. economy to have added 115,000 jobs last month.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) and the S&P 500 Index (SPX) were down 0.43% and 0.09%, respectively, while those on the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.04%, at 5:31 a.m. EDT on July 2.

Oil prices remained under pressure amid easing Middle East tensions. At the time of writing, Brent crude (CM:BZ) was down 0.78% at $70.35 a barrel, while WTI crude (CM:CL) fell 0.65% to $68.03.

During Wednesday's regular session, the Nasdaq Composite fell 0.66% and the S&P 500 slipped 0.22%, weighed down by sharp declines in chip stocks. Micron (MU) and SanDisk (SNDK) plunged more than 10%, while Intel (INTC) fell over 9%. Meanwhile, the Dow Jones briefly hit a new intraday record before ending little changed.

In fixed-income markets, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield stood around 4.49% as of the last check. Additionally, the spot gold price in U.S. dollars traded around $4,072.51 per ounce on Thursday.

In key economic reports, investors will monitor the June non-farm payrolls report, the unemployment rate, initial jobless claims, and May factory orders.

Elsewhere, major European indexes traded higher as gains in defensive sectors, including healthcare and consumer goods, offset weakness in AI-related technology stocks.

Asia-Pacific Markets Close Mixed

Asia-Pacific markets closed mixed as weakness in semiconductor stocks weighed on sentiment.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index rose 0.76%. In China, the Shanghai Composite dropped 2.03% while the Shenzhen Component declined 2.09%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei fell 2.47% and the Topix closed near the flatline.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Treasury Selects State Street's SPYM as Default ETF for Trump Accounts; Vanguard and BlackRock ETFs Are Alternatives
data.description:
The U.S. Department of the Treasury announced the investment lineup for Trump Accounts, with the State Street SPDR ​Portfolio S&P 500 ETF (SPYM) selected as the default investment exchange-traded fund (ETF) during the launch of the program. The Treasury picked BlackRock's (BLK) iShares Core S&P 500 ETF (IVV) and iShares Core S&P Total U.S. Stock Market ETF (ITOT), Vanguard Total Stock Market ETF (VTI), and the State Street SPDR Portfolio S&P 1500 Composite Stock Market ETF (SPTM) as additional investment options.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

200% short exposure to NVDA with NVDS

More on SPYM and Other ETFs Selected for Trump Accounts

Trump Accounts are tax-advantaged investment accounts that aim to help families build long-term wealth for their children. Once a Trump Account is set up, eligible children (born between 2025 and 2028) will receive a one-time contribution of $1,000 from the federal government. Moreover, families can contribute up to $5,000 per year. These funds will generally be inaccessible before age 18, when the account will convert into a traditional IRA.

At launch on July 4, all contributions to Trump Accounts will be invested in the SPYM ETF, a low-cost fund that tracks the performance of the S&P 500 Index (SPX). Treasury said that the SPYM ETF was selected to provide broad exposure to the U.S. stock market while maintaining expenses well below the statutory fee limitation. In the months ahead, Treasury will allow parents or guardians to choose how to allocate funds across the additional ETF options. Until then, all contributions to Trump Accounts will remain invested in the default SPYM ETF.

According to TipRanks' unique ETF analyst consensus, determined based on a weighted average of analyst ratings on its holdings, SPYM earns a Strong Buy rating. The average SPYM ETF price target of $105.38 indicates 20.1% upside potential. SPYM has risen 10% year-to-date. Currently, Nvidia (NVDA), Apple (AAPL), and Microsoft (MSFT) are the top three holdings of the SPYM ETF.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: CrowdStrike Stock Has Plunged 75% in Pre-Market Today—But Don't Panic Just Yet
data.description:
Seeing a massive, triple-digit tech stock suddenly lose three-quarters of its value before the opening bell is enough to give any investor a minor heart attack. On Thursday morning, ticker screens showed CrowdStrike's (CRWD) stock trading around $185 per share, a steep drop from its previous closing price of nearly $743.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

However, this is not a catastrophic crash, a devastating data breach, or a surprise earnings disaster. This wild price change is actually a perfectly timed, intentional corporate maneuver that makes the cybersecurity leader a lot more accessible to regular investors.

CrowdStrike Splits its Shares 4-for-1

The dramatic shift on your screen is the direct result of CrowdStrike executing its first-ever 4-for-1 stock split, which officially goes live today, July 2, 2026. This corporate action simply multiplies the total number of available shares by four, while simultaneously cutting the price of each individual share down to exactly one-quarter of what it used to be.

If you owned a single share of CrowdStrike stock worth $742 when the market closed on Wednesday, your account now holds four separate shares worth roughly $185 each. Because the total value of your investment stays exactly the same, the 75% drop is just a visual recalculation on the stock exchange rather than an actual loss of wealth.

Investors Cheer CrowdStrike's Growth Story

The business originally announced this split back on June 3, alongside an impressive first-quarter financial report. CrowdStrike management decided to slice the share price to appeal to everyday retail investors who might find a $700-plus price tag too expensive to buy into.

The company's business health remains incredibly strong. Wall Street analysts have been raising their target prices for CrowdStrike over the past few weeks, pointing to booming demand for its artificial intelligence security platforms and a steady pile of new recurring revenue. Now that the split has lowered the barrier to entry, more retail buyers can easily jump into the stock, keeping its strong market momentum alive.

Is CrowdStrike a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

CrowdStrike stock (CRWD) continues to carry a Strong Buy consensus, based on 37 analyst ratings over the past three months. Out of those, 29 call it a Buy, while seven recommend a Hold, and one suggests a Sell.

The average 12-month CRWD price target sits at $196.02, which represents 74.6% downside risk. (See CRWD stock forecast)
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Tesla Stock (TSLA) Drops as Safety Fears Persist, Even With Another Probe Closed
data.description:
Shares of electric vehicle giant Tesla (TSLA) edged lower during Thursday's pre-market session despite the U.S. auto safety watchdog closing another of its probes into the company's vehicles. This comes as concerns about the safety of the automaker's vehicles remain.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

TSLQ: built for a short position on TSLA

NHTSA Closes 2022 Tesla Investigation

On Thursday, the U.S. National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) announced that it had shut down its preliminary probe from 2022 into 695,000 Tesla vehicles. The regulator said the sudden deceleration incidents that sparked the investigation had dwindled to a trickle.

The investigation had covered Tesla's Model 3 and Model Y vehicles. However, NHTSA found that the defect did not cause the vehicles to drift out of their lanes. The vehicles also did not lose enough space behind the next car to cause an accident.

Moreover, in early 2022, Tesla released software updates to fix the bug causing the incident, NHTSA said. The new closure comes just days after the safety watchdog similarly closed its July 2023 probe into certain Model 3 and Model Y vehicles. The investigation had focused on power steering loss in the vehicles.

Tesla Continues to Face Scrutiny over Vehicle Safety

Meanwhile, despite the recent closures of investigations into Tesla vehicles, the EV giant has continued to face questions about the safety of its technology.

Last month, two U.S. senators called on the NHTSA to review crash data involving Tesla's driver-assist software. The call came after Reuters reported that the automaker widely inflates the safety numbers of its Full Self-Driving (FSD) technology.

Moreover, the NHTSA has been investigating Tesla's FSD systems since 2024. It launched a probe into 2.88 million units in October 2025 and escalated it in March over concerns about the FSD system's performance in poor weather conditions.

Is Tesla Stock a Buy or Sell Today?

On Wall Street, Tesla's shares now carry a Moderate Buy consensus rating from analysts. This is based on 11 Buys, 14 Holds, and three Sells issued by 28 analysts over the past three months.

However, the average TSLA price target of $403.66 suggests about 5% downside risk from current trading levels.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Bending Spoons Stock (BSP) Pops 40% after IPO — Here's Why Investors Are Buying
data.description:

data.title: 'Utterly False,' Musk Denies SpaceX AI Phone Report, Stock Tumbles
data.description:
Elon Musk denied a Wall Street Journal report claiming that SpaceX (SPCX) was developing an AI-powered handset-like device. Musk's "utterly false" response on X sent SPCX stock down 7.8% on Wednesday. The space exploration company's shares have given up most of their post-IPO gains and are now down 2.1% since listing.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

High conviction SPCX bulls now have this Tradr ETF

According to people familiar with the matter, the prototype runs on a custom operating system and integrates xAI technology. The WSJ report also said that the AI device uses Qualcomm's (QCOM) Snapdragon chipset, which sent the stock briefly higher before Musk denied claims. The AI-powered handset is also sleeker than Apple's (AAPL) iPhone.

The device was reportedly shown to some investors ahead of the company's planned initial public offering (IPO) in June. However, the report cautioned that the project was in an early stage and could be dropped completely.

Can SpaceX Expand into Smartphones?

SpaceX has invested billions of dollars to expand beyond its rocket launches and Starlink satellite internet business. It is investing heavily in AI infrastructure and integrating xAI's Grok AI model into its broader ecosystem. The company also envisions space-based data centers as Musk aims to make SpaceX a major player in the AI industry.

Although Musk denied the Wall Street Journal report, speculation about a SpaceX phone has surfaced before. Reuters reported in February that SpaceX was considering developing a mobile device that would connect directly to its Starlink satellite internet network and could compete with smartphones. However, Musk also dismissed those reports.

Meanwhile, in January, Musk had said that a Starlink phone was "not out of the question" in the future. He added that if SpaceX builds one, it would be very different from today's smartphones.

Moreover, Reuters recently reported that SpaceX is exploring plans to offer a mobile network and is in talks with Charter Communications (CHTR) to use its ground infrastructure for phone traffic. The company already works with T-Mobile (TMUS) to provide direct-to-cell satellite connectivity through its Starlink network.

Is It Too Late to Buy SpaceX?

Analysts remain divided on SpaceX's long-term outlook after its massive IPO rally. On TipRanks, SPCX has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on four Buys, three Holds, and one Sell rating. The average SpaceX price target of $216.83 implies 37.6% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth
